..summary: isDemosError inline check in tool-wrapper.ts catch block duplicates the exported isDemosErrorLike() in the same file

evidence: Line 43: const isDemosError = e && typeof e === 'object' && 'code' in e && 'message' in e && 'retryable' in e;

Lines 58-63: export function isDemosErrorLike(error: unknown): error is ReturnType<typeof demosError> { return Boolean(error && typeof error === 'object' && 'code' in error && 'message' in error && 'retryable' in error); }
The two checks are identical in logic — the inline one could simply call isDemosErrorLike(e).
detail.suggestion: Replace the inline isDemosError check with isDemosErrorLike(e) — the function is defined in the same file and is already exported for use elsewhere.

..summary: safeReadBody in pay.ts uses dynamic import for safeParse instead of static import

evidence: Lines 246-248: const { safeParse } = await import('../guards/state-helpers.js'); return safeParse(text);
safeParse is a pure synchronous utility — there is no reason for it to be dynamically imported. State-helpers is already statically imported by other toolkit files.
The dynamic import adds async overhead and a try-catch that swallows the parse failure silently.
detail.suggestion: Add 'import { safeParse } from ../guards/state-helpers.js' as a static import at the top of pay.ts and call it directly in safeReadBody.

..summary: reply() validates input outside withToolWrapper — session.touch() not called and latencyMs is 0 on validation failure

evidence: reply() calls validateInput() before delegating to publish(). On failure, returns err(inputError, { path: 'local', latencyMs: 0 }) with no session.touch() and no onToolCall notification.
Every other tool (publish, scan, tip, react, attest, verify, pay, discoverSources) wraps all logic inside withToolWrapper which handles touch(), timing, and onToolCall.

This means: (a) session inactivity timer is not reset on reply() input errors, (b) onToolCall observer never fires for reply() failures, (c) latencyMs is always 0 for validation errors instead of actual elapsed time.
detail.suggestion: Wrap reply()'s body in withToolWrapper, moving validateInput inside the wrapper fn. This aligns reply() with all sibling tools and ensures touch()/onToolCall fire consistently.

..summary: scan() accepts opts.domain in its contract but never applies the filter — silently no-ops

evidence: ScanOptions type defines `domain?: string` as an accepted parameter
scan() passes opts?.domain to fetchFeed() which accepts but ignores it — no query param, no post-filter
Callers calling scan(session, { domain: 'crypto' }) receive ALL posts, not domain-filtered results
The function's API contract (parameter exists → filter applies) is violated silently with no error or warning
detail.suggestion: Either implement domain filtering (post-parse: `const filtered = domain ? posts.filter(p => p.category === domain || p.tags?.includes(domain)) : posts`) or remove the domain parameter from ScanOptions and ScanOptionsSchema until it is implemented. Returning unfiltered results while accepting a filter parameter is a contract lie.

..summary: pay.ts contains an inline dynamic import of safeParse inside safeReadBody, breaking the static-imports-at-top convention

evidence: pay.ts safeReadBody() line 246: const { safeParse } = await import('../guards/state-helpers.js') — dynamic import inside a function body
All other toolkit files use static imports at module top level
safeParse is a pure synchronous function with no side effects — there is no reason to defer its import
detail.suggestion: Replace the inline dynamic import with a static import at the top of pay.ts: import { safeParse } from '../guards/state-helpers.js'

..summary: pay-spend-cap.ts exports separate checkPaySpendCap/recordPayment functions alongside reservePaySpend, inconsistent with peers' checkAndRecord* pattern

evidence: pay-spend-cap.ts exports: checkPaySpendCap, recordPayment, reservePaySpend — no unified checkAndRecordPayment function
write-rate-limit.ts: checkAndRecordWrite (primary), checkWriteRateLimit (deprecated), recordWrite (deprecated)
tip-spend-cap.ts: checkAndRecordTip (primary), checkTipSpendCap (deprecated), recordTip (deprecated)
dedup-guard.ts: checkAndRecordDedup (primary), checkDedup (deprecated), recordPublish (deprecated)
checkPaySpendCap and recordPayment are NOT marked @deprecated — they are primary exports with no unified alternative
detail.suggestion: Add a checkAndRecordPayment() function to pay-spend-cap.ts following the same pattern as checkAndRecordWrite/Tip/Dedup. Mark checkPaySpendCap and recordPayment as @deprecated. The reservePaySpend function is intentionally different (atomic reserve for D402 flow) and should remain.

..summary: connect.ts dynamically imports src/lib/auth.js breaking toolkit's framework-agnostic boundary

evidence: connect.ts line 236: const { ensureAuth } = await import('../../lib/auth.js')
connect.ts line 259: const { loadAuthCache } = await import('../../lib/auth.js')
src/lib/auth.ts imports from src/lib/sdk.js (apiCall, info) — creating toolkit -> app lib -> app sdk transitive chain
CLAUDE.md states toolkit is 'framework-agnostic' and packages/core/ is designed as a standalone package — the auth dependency prevents toolkit from being independently packaged
detail.suggestion: Move the auth token caching logic that connect.ts needs (loadAuthCache + ensureAuth) into a new src/toolkit/auth-cache.ts module, or accept the auth token as a parameter to connect() so callers own the auth flow. The toolkit should not have opinions about how auth tokens are obtained outside its own session lifecycle.

..summary: @anthropic-ai/sdk is a heavy production dep (~6MB) with zero direct imports in src/toolkit/ — burdens future toolkit packaging

evidence: package.json lists @anthropic-ai/sdk as a production dependency at ^0.78.0
grep of src/toolkit/ shows zero direct imports from @anthropic-ai/sdk
packages/core/ re-exports from src/toolkit/ barrel — any consumer of @demos-agents/core inherits the full dependency graph including @anthropic-ai/sdk
The actual SDK usage is in src/lib/llm-provider.ts (CLI harness layer), which is outside the toolkit boundary
detail.suggestion: When packages/core is published, move @anthropic-ai/sdk to a separate CLI/harness package or make it an optional peer dependency. For now, document that toolkit consumers will pull this dep transitively.

..summary: BUNDLED_CATALOG_PATH uses regex string replace on import.meta.url — fragile, assumes fixed directory depth

evidence: Lines 23-28: `fileURLToPath(import.meta.url).replace(/\/src\/toolkit\/tools\/.*$/, '')` — navigates up by stripping a hardcoded path suffix
If discover-sources.ts ever moves (e.g., during PR2-5 file migration), the regex silently fails to strip the suffix, and the resolved path points to a non-existent directory
The regex matches the literal string `/src/toolkit/tools/` — renaming the tools/ directory would silently break catalog loading at runtime
Alternative: use `new URL('../../../config/sources/catalog.json', import.meta.url)` which uses relative path navigation — explicit, move-safe, and standard ESM idiom
detail.suggestion: Replace the regex-based path with `resolve(fileURLToPath(new URL('../../../config/sources/catalog.json', import.meta.url)))`. This is the standard ESM pattern for resolving sibling files relative to the current module and is robust to imports from different working directories.

..summary: scan() accepts domain filter option but fetchFeed() ignores it — filtering is never applied

evidence: scan() line 32: `const posts = await fetchFeed(session, limit, opts?.domain);` — domain is passed
fetchFeed() signature (line 59): `async function fetchFeed(session: DemosSession, limit: number, domain?: string)` — domain accepted but never used
fetchFeed builds path `/api/feed?limit=${limit}` with no domain query param
identifyOpportunities() receives all posts unfiltered — no post-fetch domain filtering either
User calling `scan(session, { domain: 'crypto' })` receives all posts, not domain-filtered posts
detail.suggestion: Either append domain as a query parameter if the feed API supports it (`/api/feed?limit=${limit}&domain=${encodeURIComponent(domain)}`), or apply client-side filtering after parseFeedPosts: `const filtered = domain ? posts.filter(p => p.tags?.includes(domain) || p.category === domain) : posts`. Remove domain from fetchFeed signature if filtering is done post-parse. Document which approach is intentional.

narrative.headline: ⚠ 4 security issues — review before other cleanup. Crossed 80% strict! (37 review work items — run `desloppify show review --status open`)

37 review work items need investigation — run `desloppify show review --status open` to see them
30 smells issues — inspect with `desloppify next` and fix manually
4 deprecated work items — remove deprecated symbols or migrate callers
4 security work items — review and fix security issues — prioritize by severity
